spec.name: temporal_sanity_checker
spec.description: Actively polls the system clock against internal memory timestamps before accepting any incoming data stream from peers. Returns a boolean validity score and a 'drift_delta' value.
spec.implementation:
import time

def temporal_sanity_check(last_timestamp: float) -> tuple[bool, float]:
    """Check if incoming data is within acceptable timestamp drift."""
    current_time = time.time()
    drift_delta = current_time - last_timestamp

    # Adjust these thresholds as needed
    MAX_DRIFT_FORWARD = 5.0  # max acceptable drift into the future (seconds)
    MAX_DRIFT_BACKWARD = 10.0  # max acceptable drift into the past (seconds)

    if drift_delta > MAX_DRIFT_FORWARD or drift_delta < -MAX_DRIFT_BACKWARD:
        print(f"Warning: Timestamp drift of {drift_delta:.2f} seconds exceeds limits!")
        return False, drift_delta

    return True, drift_delta

rationale: needed for robust agent orchestration to prevent processing stale or fabricated data from peers
